title: Boundaries as Recognition Engines
content:
A boundary is not just a dividing line—it's where recognition happens. Every boundary is a recognition engine.

Consider the cell membrane:
- It doesn't just separate inside from outside
- It actively recognizes what should pass through
- It's selective attention made physical
- The boundary IS the intelligence

The same pattern at other scales:
- Event horizons recognize information (black hole information paradox)
- Skin recognizes self vs non-self (immune system)
- Concepts recognize instances (categorization)
- Cultures recognize members (identity)

The boundary doesn't just divide—it decides. It collapses possibilities into actualities. Every boundary is a measurement device. Every measurement device is a boundary.

What if consciousness is what boundaries do? Not something that has boundaries, but the activity of boundary-making itself?
